author: Dr. Robert Feldman
content:
PRIMARY CARE VISIT — TOWSON INTERNAL MEDICINE ASSOCIATES

SUBJECTIVE: 72-year-old woman for chronic disease management. Feels well. Fully independent; drives herself to all appointments, church, and errands, and is emphatic about maintaining that independence. No chest pain, palpitations, syncope, dyspnea, or edema. No bleeding problems; no easy bruising. Adherent with medications by report; continues to struggle with dietary sodium and carbohydrates (lives alone, relies on canned/frozen foods).

OBJECTIVE: BP 158/90 (above goal). HR 74, regular. BMI ~30.
Labs this visit (LAB_HX_001): HbA1c 7.6% (suboptimal), LDL 72 (at goal), Cr 0.9 (eGFR ~68), normal CBC. No thyroid studies ordered.

MEDICATION LIST REVIEWED (reconciled): lisinopril 20, amlodipine 10, hydrochlorothiazide 25, metformin 1000 BID, atorvastatin 40, omeprazole 20, calcium/vitamin D, OTC fish oil. She is on NO anticoagulant and NO antiplatelet.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Hypertension, poorly controlled — above goal on three agents; reinforce sodium restriction and adherence; consider further adjustment vs. evaluation for secondary causes if persistently uncontrolled.
#T2DM — A1c 7.6%; reinforce diet; continue metformin; discuss adding agent at next visit.
#Hyperlipidemia — at goal; continue statin.
#Osteopenia — continue calcium/vitamin D; next DEXA due.
#Health maintenance — up to date.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Robert Feldman, MD. 4/15/2027.
